content.problemStatement:
Chef has fallen in love with Cheffina, and wants to buy NNN gifts for her. On reaching the gift shop, Chef got to know the following two things:


The cost of each gift is 111 coin.
On the purchase of every 4th4^{th}4th gift, Chef gets the 5th5^{th}5th gift free of cost.
What is the minimum number of coins that Chef will require in order to come out of the shop carrying NNN gifts?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT — the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN, the number of gifts in the shop.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of coins that Chef will require to obtain all NNN gifts.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the number of gifts Chef wants to take.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of coins Chef has to spend to obtain N gifts.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=5, one gift is free, so Chef pays for 4 gifts. For N=4, no free gift is obtained, so Chef pays for all 4 gifts.

solution.approach: For every group of 5 gifts Chef pays for only 4 because the 5th is free. Hence for N gifts the number of free gifts is floor(N/5). The required coins = N - floor(N/5). This can also be written as (N/5)*4 + N%5 using integer division. The computation is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.
